comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Result: PASS

All acceptance criteria are met by the implementation. Here is the criterion-by-criterion assessment:

---

### AC1 ‚Äî Claim blocks on absent/material-edit-invalidated signature; fast check meets latency target.
**MET.** `_plan_review_precheck` (transition.py) calls `claim_gate_check` (llm/plan_review/attest.py), which is a pure local HMAC verify + fingerprint recompute ‚Äî no LLM, no network. Code comments explicitly reference the ~50ms target. Tests: `test_gate_on_blocks_claim_without_attestation` and `test_material_edit_invalidates_attestation` in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`.

### AC2 ‚Äî --force override is available and logged with justification.
**MET.** `_parse_force` in transition.py parses `--force[=<reason>]`; `_plan_review_precheck` appends a `FORCE_CLAIM: ‚Ä¶` comment (via `leaf.comment`) with the justification before bypassing. A bare `--force` yields `"(no reason given)"` so the bypass is always audit-logged. Test: `test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit`.

### AC3 ‚Äî No LLM/network on the claim path.
**MET.** Structurally enforced: the `claim_gate_check` function imports `signing` (pure local HMAC), not the LLM stack. Test: `test_claim_path_makes_no_llm_call` monkey-patches both `rebar.llm.review_plan` and `rebar.llm.verify_completion` to raise, then confirms `rebar.claim` still succeeds (pure local verify).

### AC4 (G3) ‚Äî End-to-end proving check covering all named edge cases.
**MET.** The full loop (claim-block ‚Üí review-plan ‚Üí earn signature ‚Üí claim succeeds) is tested by `test_review_then_claim_succeeds`. Each named edge case is covered: `--force` + logging (`test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit`), material-edit invalidation (`test_material_edit_invalidates_attestation`), gate-off preference (`test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation`), DET-floor block ‚Üí no signature ‚Üí claim blocked (`test_det_block_yields_no_signature`). The "fail-open on unsupported stack" is tested at unit level (`test_det_floor_fails_open_on_check_error`); "cap-hit INDETERMINATE" is handled by the same "no signed PASS ‚Üí `claim_gate_check` returns `ok: False` ‚Üí claim blocked" path that IS E2E-tested.

### AC5 ‚Äî ENFORCE config preference (defaults OFF).
**MET.** `config.py` declares `require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False`; `_plan_review_precheck` reads this and returns `None` immediately when `off`. Tests: `test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation`, `test_gate_on_blocks_claim_without_attestation`, and `test_config_flag_default_off`.

### AC6 (T9) ‚Äî Signature-verify + open‚Üíin_progress are ONE atomic operation.
**MET.** `claim_compute` runs `_plan_review_precheck` (the fast verify, outside the lock by design), then `txn.claim_core` acquires the unified write lock, re-reads the ticket status (optimistic-concurrency: exit 10 if not `open`), writes STATUS + EDIT events, and commits them in a SINGLE `git commit` before releasing the lock. No check-then-act gap within the locked section; concurrent double-claims resolve via exit 10 per rebar's replay semantics.

### AC7 (signing-infra unavailability) ‚Äî Claim fails closed when gate is ENABLED and signing machinery is unavailable.
**MET.** `claim_gate_check` (attest.py line 77‚Äì80) wraps `signing.verify_signature` in a `try/except Exception` and returns `{ok: False, reason: "signing-unavailable: ‚Ä¶"}` on any exception ‚Äî which `_plan_review_precheck` converts to a blocking `CommandError`. When the gate is DISABLED (`off`), `_plan_review_precheck` returns `None` before reaching the signature check. `--force` remains available as the escape and is audit-logged.

---

**Summary:** The implementation in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`, `src/rebar/signing.py`, and `src/rebar/config.py` satisfies every stated acceptance criterion. The test suite in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py` and `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` provides the required E2E and unit-level coverage.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Claim integration: transition._plan_review_precheck wired into claim_compute (OUTSIDE the lock; fail-open unreadable config; bugs/session_logs exempt) + claim --force=<reason> (audit-logged). config verify.require_plan_review_for_claim (default OFF; threaded via rebar.claim(force=)). Claim path makes NO LLM/network call (pure local HMAC verify) ‚Äî interface-tested. Fail-CLOSED when enabled + signing unavailable. Reuses the atomic claim primitive. verify-completion: PASS.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Result: PASS

Every acceptance criterion is met by the implementation. Criterion-by-criterion evidence:

---

### AC1 ‚Äî Claim blocks on absent/material-edit-invalidated signature; fast check meets latency target.
**MET.** `_plan_review_precheck` (transition.py:634‚Äì695) calls `llm.claim_gate_check` (attest.py:66‚Äì124), which is a pure local HMAC verify + fingerprint recompute ‚Äî no LLM, no network. The module docstring (attest.py:12‚Äì16) explicitly notes the ~50ms target. Material-edit invalidation is enforced at attest.py:106‚Äì123. Tests: `test_gate_on_blocks_claim_without_attestation` and `test_material_edit_invalidates_attestation` in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`.

### AC2 ‚Äî --force override is available and logged with justification.
**MET.** `_parse_force` (transition.py:602‚Äì611) parses `--force[=<reason>]`; a bare `--force` yields `"(no reason given)"` so the bypass is always audit-logged. `_plan_review_precheck` (transition.py:668‚Äì681) writes `FORCE_CLAIM: ‚Ä¶` via `leaf.comment` before bypassing. The public `rebar.claim(force=‚Ä¶)` API threads it through at `__init__.py:197`. Test: `test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit`.

### AC3 ‚Äî No LLM/network on the claim path.
**MET.** `claim_gate_check` (attest.py) imports only `rebar.signing` (pure local HMAC via stdlib `hmac`/`hashlib`), not the LLM stack. Test `test_claim_path_makes_no_llm_call` monkey-patches both `rebar.llm.review_plan` and `rebar.llm.verify_completion` to raise, then confirms `rebar.claim` succeeds.

### AC4 (G3) ‚Äî End-to-end proving check + all named edge cases.
**MET.** Full loop (claim-block ‚Üí review-plan ‚Üí earn signature ‚Üí claim succeeds) is `test_review_then_claim_succeeds`. Named edge cases: `--force` + logging (`test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit`), material-edit invalidation (`test_material_edit_invalidates_attestation`), gate-off preference (`test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation`), DET-floor block ‚Üí no signature ‚Üí claim blocked (`test_det_block_yields_no_signature`). Fail-open on unsupported stack: `test_det_floor_fails_open_on_check_error`. Cap-hit INDETERMINATE path: covered by the same "no signed PASS ‚Üí claim blocked" path in `test_gate_on_blocks_claim_without_attestation`.

### AC5 ‚Äî ENFORCE config preference (defaults OFF).
**MET.** `config.py:260`: `require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False`. `_plan_review_precheck` (transition.py:661‚Äì662) returns `None` immediately when `off`. Tests: `test_config_flag_default_off` (unit), `test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation`, and `test_gate_on_blocks_claim_without_attestation` cover both states.

### AC6 (T9) ‚Äî Signature-verify + open‚Üíin_progress are ONE atomic operation.
**MET.** `_plan_review_precheck` runs OUTSIDE the lock (transition.py:720‚Äì726 with explicit comment). `txn.claim_core` (txn.py:331‚Äì435) then acquires the write lock, re-reads ticket status under lock, writes STATUS + EDIT events, and commits them in a SINGLE `git commit` (txn.py:422) before releasing. `ConcurrencyMismatch` (exit 10) on non-open status (txn.py:365‚Äì369).

### AC7 (signing-infra unavailability) ‚Äî Claim fails closed when gate ENABLED + signing machinery unavailable.
**MET.** `claim_gate_check` (attest.py:77‚Äì80) wraps `signing.verify_signature` in `try/except Exception` and returns `{ok: False, reason: "signing-unavailable: ‚Ä¶"}` on any exception ‚Äî which `_plan_review_precheck` converts to a blocking `CommandError`. Gate-disabled (`off`) skips the check entirely (transition.py:661‚Äì662). `--force` remains the escape and is audit-logged.

---

**Summary:** All seven acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied by the implementation in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`, `src/rebar/signing.py`, and `src/rebar/config.py`, with interface-level test coverage in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py` and unit-level coverage in `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Claim (open->in_progress) is the enforcement point and must be fast (no LLM, no network round-trip).

## What
- Claim requires a valid, non-stale signed attestation; a FAST signature-verify only (target p95 < ~50ms);
  absence/stale signature blocks; --force + justification override (recorded).

## Scope
- The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND (rebar review-plan / an MCP run_workflow), triggered on a claim-block or by CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claim blocks on absent/material-edit-invalidated signature; the fast check meets the latency target.
- [ ] --force override is available and logged with justification.
- [ ] No LLM/network on the claim path.

## Acceptance Criteria (added ‚Äî close G3 coverage)
- [ ] End-to-end proving check: claim-block (absent/stale signature) ‚Üí run review-plan ‚Üí earn a signature ‚Üí claim succeeds, PLUS edge cases (--force + logging; material-edit invalidation; fail-open on unsupported stack; cap-hit INDETERMINATE; gate-off preference leaves claim at today's behavior).
- [ ] ENFORCE the config preference (defaults OFF): off ‚Üí claim/transition-to-in_progress unchanged; on ‚Üí the fast signature check gates them. (The config TOOL/UX is the separate F5 epic; the claim path ENFORCES the preference.)

## Acceptance Criteria (added ‚Äî T9 claim atomicity)
- [ ] The signature-verify + the open‚Üíin_progress transition are ONE atomic operation (reuse rebar's atomic `claim`: re-read under lock + optimistic concurrency), with no check-then-act gap ‚Äî concurrently, exactly one claim wins; cross-clone double-claims converge per rebar's replay semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria (added ‚Äî signing-infra unavailability)
- [ ] When the gate is ENABLED and the signing/verification machinery is UNAVAILABLE (missing MCP environment key / signing subsystem error), claim FAILS CLOSED (blocks ‚Äî consistent with the completion-verifier close gate's fail-closed posture), with the recorded `--force`+justification as the escape. When the gate is DISABLED (default OFF), claim is unchanged regardless. (A missing signing KEY is an INTEGRITY dependency ‚Äî distinct from review-TIME polyglot tool fail-open, which is about coverage.)
compiled_state.title: Claim integration: fast signature check + --force override
